heading: Compensation for Cuban interference with broadcasting in United States
label: 1465e Compensation for Cuban interference with broadcasting in United States
notes: Repeal of SectionSection repealed upon transmittal of determination by President under that democratically elected government in Cuba is in power, see .section 6063(c)(3) of this titlesection 6037(c) of this title

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Radio Broadcasting to Cuba Act which comprises this subchapter, and not as part of the United States Information and Educational Exchange Act of 1948 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Interim assistance to United States broadcasters
children.label: a Interim assistance to United States broadcasters
children.text: It is the intent of the Congress that the Secretary of State should seek prompt and full settlement of United States claims against the Government of Cuba arising from Cuban interference with broadcasting in the United States. Pending the settlement of these claims, it is appropriate to provide some interim assistance to the United States broadcasters who are adversely affected by Cuban radio interference and who seek to assert their right to measures to counteract the effects of such interference.

children.heading: Money payments pursuant to authority from Federal Communications Commission
children.label: b Money payments pursuant to authority from Federal Communications Commission
children.text: Accordingly, the Board may make payments to the United States radio broadcasting station licensees upon their application for expenses which they have incurred before, on, or after , in mitigating, pursuant to special temporary authority from the Federal Communications Commission, the effects of activities by the Government of Cuba which directly interfere with the transmission or reception of broadcasts by these licensees. Such expenses shall be limited to the costs of equipment replaced (less depreciation) and associated technical and engineering costs.October 4, 1983

children.heading: Regulations and procedures
children.label: c Regulations and procedures
children.text: The Federal Communications Commission shall issue such regulations and establish such procedures for carrying out this section as the Federal Communications Commission finds appropriate. Such regulations shall be issued no later than one hundred and eighty days after .October 4, 1983

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Board, $5,000,000 for use in compensating United States radio broadcasting licensees pursuant to this section. Amounts appropriated under this section are authorized to be available until expended.

children.heading: Four-year availability for appropriated funds
children.label: e Four-year availability for appropriated funds
children.text: Funds appropriated for implementation of this section shall be available for a period of no more than four years following the initial broadcast occurring as a result of programs described in this subchapter.

children.heading: Presidential task force
children.label: f Presidential task force
children.text: It is the sense of the Congress that the President should establish a task force to analyze the level of interference from the operation of Cuban radio stations experienced by broadcasters in the United States and to seek a practical political and technical solution to this problem.

children.heading: Effective Date
children.label: g Effective Date
children.text: This section shall enter into effect on .October 1, 1984
